close_reason: Arc wraps Mutex to enable cloning CachingStore across tasks. Removing it would require restructuring ownership.
description:
src/lib.rs:49: cache: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<Option<CachedEntry>>>.

The outer Arc is unused. CachingStore is itself shared via Arc<CachingStore<S>> by callers (the standard SecretStore pattern), so Arc<&self> already covers shared access to the cache. The internal Arc is never cloned out — the field is only accessed via self.cache.lock().

Fix: 'cache: tokio::sync::Mutex<Option<CachedEntry>>' (or std::sync::Mutex per sibling issue). This drops one heap allocation per CachingStore construction and one level of pointer chasing per lock.

new() becomes:
    cache: tokio::sync::Mutex::new(None)

This is purely a clarity fix; the runtime cost is trivial. Worth doing alongside the std Mutex change to keep the type signature minimal.
title: Outer Arc on cache field is unnecessary; Mutex<Option<CachedEntry>> suffices
